get.description:
Get SAML Request

 Get SAML Request details by ID. Returns details that are parsed from the application's SAML Request.

 Required permissions:
   - `session.read`

parameters.name: saml_request_id
parameters.description: ID of the SAML Request, as obtained from the redirect URL.

schema.title: saml_request_id
schema.description: ID of the SAML Request, as obtained from the redirect URL.

post.description:
Create Response

 Finalize a SAML Request and get the response definition for success or failure.
 The response must be handled as per the SAML definition to inform the application about the success or failure.
 On success, the response contains details for the application to obtain the SAMLResponse.
 This method can only be called once for an SAML request.

 Required permissions:
   - `session.link`

sequence.title: sequence
sequence.description:
sequence represents the order of events. It's always counting

 on read: the sequence of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
change_date is the timestamp when the object was changed

 on read: the timestamp of the last event reduced by the projection

 on manipulation: the timestamp of the event(s) added by the manipulation

resourceOwner.title: resource_owner
resourceOwner.description: resource_owner is the organization or instance_id an object belongs to

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: creation_date is the timestamp where the first operation on the object was made

error.title: error
error.description: Set this field when the authorization flow failed. It creates a response depending on the SP, with the error details set.

session.title: session
session.description: The session of the authenticated user, to finalize the authorization request.

post.title: post
post.description: Set if the binding is POST-Binding, where the application expects to be called per HTTP POST with the SAMLResponse and RelayState in the form body.

redirect.title: redirect
redirect.description: Set if the binding is Redirect-Binding, where the user can directly be redirected to the application, using a \"302 FOUND\" status to the URL.

url.title: url
url.description: URL including the Assertion Consumer Service where the user should be redirected or has to call per POST, depending on the binding. Contains details for the application to obtain the response on success, or error details on failure. Note that this field must be treated as credentials, as the contained SAMLResponse or code can be used on behalve of the user.

samlRequestId.title: saml_request_id
samlRequestId.description: ID of the SAML Request, as obtained from the redirect URL.

relayState.title: relay_state
relayState.description: The SAML RelaySate, that needs to be returned to the application to match the response to the request.

samlResponse.title: saml_response
samlResponse.description: The SAML Response, that needs to be returned to the application to complete the SAML flow.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: Time when the SAMLRequest was created.

issuer.title: issuer
issuer.description: SAML entityID of the application that created the SAMLRequest.

assertionConsumerService.title: assertion_consumer_service
assertionConsumerService.description: URL which points back to the assertion consumer service of the application that created the SAMLRequest.

relayState.title: relay_state
relayState.description: RelayState provided by the application for the request.

binding.title: binding
binding.description: Binding used by the application for the request.

zitadel.saml.v2.SAMLRequest.title: SAMLRequest
zitadel.saml.v2.SAMLRequest.description:
SAMLRequest contains information about a SAML authentication request.
 see: https://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/Post2.0/sstc-saml-tech-overview-2.0.html

sessionId.title: session_id
sessionId.description: ID of the session, used to login the user. Connects the session to the SAML Request.

sessionToken.title: session_token
sessionToken.description: Token to verify the session is valid.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

google.protobuf.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message.
